Legal · Terms

Terms of Service

Last updated: 28 April 2026. These terms govern your use of tamazia.co.uk and any preliminary engagement with Tamazia preceding a signed Statement of Work. Formal client engagements are governed by the executed engagement letter, which prevails over these terms in case of conflict.

1. Who we are

Tamazia is an international SEO and regulatory-compliance content firm. Founder and principal: Aman Pareek, LLM in International Business Law (King’s College London). The firm operates cross-jurisdictionally with delivery in the United Kingdom, European Union, United States, GCC (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain), Singapore, and Hong Kong.

2. Use of this website

You may use tamazia.co.uk for personal, business, or research purposes provided you:

  • Do not attempt to disrupt, scan, or stress-test the infrastructure.
  • Do not scrape content for republication without written permission.
  • Do not submit false or misleading information through any form.
  • Comply with applicable laws of the jurisdiction from which you access the site.

3. Submissions and enquiries

Information you submit through forms on this site (Quick Audit, briefing request, regulatory briefings signup) is processed under our privacy notice. Submitting an enquiry does not create a client relationship. A client relationship is created only by signed engagement letter or Statement of Work executed by Tamazia.

4. Audit output and recommendations

Audit outputs delivered through this site (whether free, sample, or paid) are professional opinions based on the data available at the time of analysis. They are not legal advice and are not a substitute for guidance from your retained counsel or compliance team. Tamazia may reference regulatory frameworks such as UK GDPR, EU GDPR, HIPAA, SEC Reg FD, MiFID II, ICC Rules, LCIA Rules, SRA Standards, FDA Title 21, and others. Each reference reflects publicly available regulator text current at the time of audit; regulator interpretations evolve and should be verified before action.

5. Intellectual property

All content on tamazia.co.uk, including copy, design, code, and brand marks, is the intellectual property of Tamazia or its licensors. Audit reports and deliverables produced for paying clients are governed by the licence terms in the executed engagement letter.

6. Confidentiality

Information you share in support of a paid engagement is treated as confidential and processed only for the purpose of that engagement. Standard professional-confidentiality obligations apply. We are happy to execute a mutual non-disclosure agreement before sensitive disclosures.

7. Third-party services and links

Insights articles and audit outputs may reference third-party regulators, publications, or tools. Such references do not constitute endorsement. Tamazia is not responsible for the content of external sites linked from this domain.

8. Liability

tamazia.co.uk is provided "as is" for informational purposes. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Tamazia is not liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, or exemplary damages arising from use of this website or reliance on its content. Liability for paid engagements is governed by the engagement letter and its express limitation-of-liability clauses.

9. Governing law and disputes

These website terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Disputes arising from use of this website are subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales. For disputes arising under a paid engagement, the governing-law and dispute-resolution clauses of the executed engagement letter prevail (typically arbitration under ICC, LCIA, SIAC, or DIAC Rules depending on the engagement).

10. Changes

Material changes to these terms are versioned by the "Last updated" date at the top. Continued use of tamazia.co.uk after a change constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

11. Contact

Questions about these terms: realfamemedia@gmail.com. For data-subject requests under UK GDPR / EU GDPR / UAE PDPL, see the privacy notice.

Read alongside the privacy notice and cookie policy. Engagement letters and Statements of Work govern paid relationships.

REGULATORY BRIEFINGS

Receive a quarterly briefing on regulatory developments affecting your sector. No marketing, no sales. Findings only.

By submitting you consent to receive regulatory briefings from Tamazia. Unsubscribe at any time. No marketing or sales content.
